channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Women's Voluntary Service Collected Kitchen Waste for Pig Food in Britain During World War II
item.description: During World War II, Britain faced severe food shortages due to German U-boat attacks disrupting imports. To maximize domestic food production, particularly meat from pigs, the government launched sal...

item.title: Investigation reveals delays and lenient consequences for Canadian lawyers involved in child sexual misconduct
item.description: An investigation by the National Post’s Investigative Journalism Bureau reviewed 39 cases since 2000 in which Canadian lawyers and judges were convicted or disciplined for child sexual offences or mis...

item.title: Shepherd Honoured with Sitara-i-Shujaat for Sacrificing Life to Stop Suicide Attack in Attock
item.description: In a commendable act of bravery, a local shepherd named Liaqat lost his life while averting a potential suicide attack near a key security post in Jand tehsil, Attock. The incident took place on Monda...

item.title: A Look Back at the 1984 Bicentennial Parade in Lebanon, Pennsylvania
item.description: The 1984 Bicentennial Parade in Lebanon, Pennsylvania, marked a significant milestone as the city celebrated its 200th anniversary. Drawing thousands of local residents and visitors, the parade transf...

item.title: Three Church Leaders Killed in Kangpokpi Ambush Amid Community Tensions
item.description: In Manipur, a tragic incident occurred on Wednesday where three church leaders were killed and five others injured in an ambush while returning from a meeting in Churachandpur to Kangpokpi. The victim...

item.title: Nigeria seeks $1.25bn World Bank loan as total debt hits N159 trillion
item.description: President Bola Tinubu dey look to collect another $1.25 billion loan from the World Bank, wey go focus on investment and job creation. If the loan clear, e go push Nigeria’s external debt to around ₦7...

item.title: DOJ confirms Philippine law allows surrender of suspects to international courts
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – The Philippine Department of Justice (DOJ) has clarified that under Republic Act No. 9851, the government can surrender suspects in the country to international courts or tribuna...

item.title: Congo sanctuary protects orphaned bonobos from poaching
item.description: Located on the outskirts of Kinshasa, the capital of the Democratic Republic of Congo, a unique sanctuary provides care for orphaned bonobos rescued from poachers. Despite legal protections, these end...

item.title: Moses Kuria Critiques Gachagua for Comments on Africa Forward Summit, Questions His International Engagement
item.description: Former CS Moses Kuria has openly criticised DCP leader Rigathi Gachagua over his remarks concerning the ongoing Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi. Kuria argued that Gachagua's comments undermined Kenya...

item.title: Tanzania uses stable peace to attract investors and promote tourism
item.description: Tanzania continues to leverage its long-standing peace and political stability as a cornerstone for encouraging foreign and local investments. The government sees this stable environment as an opportu...

item.title: Mary Trump warns of threats to democracy following Virginia FBI raid and court ruling
item.description: Mary Trump, psychologist and political commentator, has raised concerns about recent actions she views as threats to democratic institutions. She highlighted the FBI raid on the office of L. Louise Lu...

item.title: The Escalating Threat of Technology-Facilitated Abuse and the Need for Stronger Safeguards
item.description: Since the advent of generative AI platforms such as ChatGPT, technology has transformed daily life in countless ways, but it has also introduced new avenues for abuse. Technology-facilitated abuse, wh...

item.title: Joe Nina pays heartfelt tribute to Maria McCloy following her passing
item.description: South African music legend Joe Nina has shared a poignant farewell to the late Maria McCloy, a prominent figure in the country’s arts and entertainment scene. The news of McCloy’s death shocked Nina, ...

item.title: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa aligns with federal government in counter-terrorism efforts
item.description: Defence Minister Khawaja Asif has stated that Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP) government is now cooperating fully with the federal government in combating terrorism. Speaking in the National Assembly, he prai...

item.title: SP leader raises questions over cause of Prateek Yadav’s death
item.description: Prateek Yadav, the younger son of former Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Mulayam Singh Yadav, passed away suddenly at the age of 38 in Lucknow on Wednesday morning. He was brought to Civil Hospital at ar...

item.title: Surulere Residents Rally Support for Desmond Elliot’s Fourth Term Bid
item.description: Naija people for Surulere don show strong support for Olusola Desmond Elliot as him dey seek fourth term for Lagos State House of Assembly. On top Tuesday morning, market leaders, elders, youth groups...

item.title: Supreme Court directs Malacañang to comment on Bato dela Rosa's petition seeking to block ICC arrest warrant
item.description: The Supreme Court of the Philippines has formally asked the Marcos administration to submit its comments on Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa's petition that seeks a temporary restraining order to preve...

item.title: Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke dies at age 29
item.description: Brandon Clarke, a professional basketball player for the NBA's Memphis Grizzlies, has tragically passed away at the age of 29. Clarke, known for his athleticism and defensive skills, played a signific...

item.title: Kenyan Teacher Calculates Her Earnings and Realises Low Pay
item.description: Kenyan teacher Cillah has captured public attention after sharing the stark reality of her earnings in a viral video. She broke down her KSh 30,000 monthly salary, calculating that it amounts to rough...

item.title: Vietnam Increases Oil Imports from Multiple Countries Following Iran Conflict
item.description: After the war for Iran break out, Vietnam don sharply increase the import of refined oil products from countries like Nigeria, Malaysia, and South Korea to cover the shortfall wey dey happen because o...
